slides.title: When Things Crash 💥
slides.content:
## Panic!

Sometimes a program hits an error so bad that it physically cannot continue (like trying to access an index outside of an array). 

In Go, this is called a **panic**. A panic will immediately stop the current function and crash the program unless we catch it.

slides.title: Recovering from Disaster 🛟
slides.content: You can 'catch' a panic using the `recover()` function inside a `defer` block. This prevents the app from crashing entirely.

quizData.question: Where must the `recover()` function be called to successfully stop a panic?
